follow_up_question: Why do you think plastic helmets were better for baseball players?

title: Kids Helping Kids: A Miracle Telethon
story: Lights, camera, action! The Children's Miracle Network Telethon took center stage for the sixth time in 1993. On June 6, kids and grown-ups across the country tuned in to watch this special TV event. They cheered, laughed, and even danced along as the show raised money to help sick children in hospitals. It was a day full of hope and kindness, showing how powerful we can be when we work together.
activity_question: What was the main purpose of the Children's Miracle Network Telethon?
activity_choices: To raise money for sick children
To launch a new TV channel
To teach kids how to dance
activity_answer: To raise money for sick children
follow_up_question: How do you think a telethon helps raise money for charity?

title: Tamil Takes Its Place Among Classical Languages
story: Languages tell the story of human history. In 2004, Tamil joined an elite group. On June 6, India's President Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam declared Tamil a Classical language. This special status was announced to both houses of India's Parliament. Tamil's rich literature and ancient roots earned it this honor, placing it alongside languages like Sanskrit and Greek.
activity_question: Who declared Tamil a Classical language?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to recognize ancient languages?
